title: Graceful Boundaries limits discovery response
description: Response shape for the limits discovery endpoint (GET /api/limits or GET /.well-known/limits) per Graceful Boundaries. Spec: https://gracefulboundaries.dev/spec

conformance.description: Self-declared conformance level. A self-assertion; external agents validate it against actual behavior.

changelog.description: URL to a changelog resource for limit changes. Must be a relative path or same-origin absolute URL.

feed.description: URL to a feed (JSON Feed, Atom, RSS) for change notifications. Must be a relative path or same-origin absolute URL.

extensions.description: Named extension discovery URLs (e.g. actionBoundaries). Each value must be a relative path or same-origin absolute URL. Presence does not affect Level 1-4 conformance.

limits.description: Map of endpoint keys to documented endpoint limits. May be empty for not-applicable or none declarations.

agentCapable.description: Reserved (roadmap): whether the endpoint is designed for machine consumption.

type.description: Limit category: ip-rate, key-rate, user-rate, global-rate, resource-dedup, cooldown, concurrency, quota, cost-limit, burst-rate, or a service-defined type.

limitId.description: Stable identifier for this specific limit.

limitType.description: Explicit copy of the limit type when multiple limits interact.

scope.description: Subject of the limit: ip, key, user, resource, or global.

costMetric.description: Resource unit for quota or cost limits: tokens, credits, bytes, dollars.

windowResetAt.description: ISO timestamp or Unix timestamp for the window reset.

returnsCached.description: For resource-dedup limits: repeat requests return the cached result as a 200 instead of a 429.
